claimText: SODAR (Sonic Detection and Ranging) was developed by CSIR-AMPRI, Bhopal.
examUse: Institution-feature or correct-pair question matching technologies with their developing agencies.
distractorHooks: Developed by the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD)

claimText: SODAR operates by transmitting sound pulses and monitoring the intensity and frequency of the returned signals.
evidenceExcerpt: Sends sound pulses to monitor intensity and frequency of returned signals.
examUse: Statement-based question on the working principle of atmospheric sensors.
distractorHooks: Uses radio waves instead of sound pulses
Monitors only the speed of the returned signal

claimText: SODAR is used to determine the wind profile and temperature structure specifically in the lower atmosphere.
evidenceExcerpt: Determines lower-atmosphere wind profile and temperature structure.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the scope and atmospheric layer targeted by SODAR.

claimText: SODAR provides data on specific atmospheric parameters such as inversion height, fog layer height, and turbulence strength.
evidenceExcerpt: Provides data on inversion height, fog layer height, and turbulence strength.
examUse: Multiple-statement question identifying the specific meteorological parameters measured by SODAR.
